hazard.summary: Framework retry logic ignored provider Retry-After semantics, causing coordinated retry amplification under shared provider contention.
signals: agent.ts applies local exponential backoff for HTTP 429 responses without consulting Retry-After.
Affected retry paths identified at lines 2039, 3267, and 5884.
isRetryableError() defaults to true, collapsing transient throttling and quota exhaustion into the same retry path.
Retry-After headers were available via responseHeaders but not consumed by retry scheduling logic.
PR #1283 adds Retry-After parsing and floors retry delay against provider cooldown hints.
Merged fix includes integration tests validating Retry-After: 30 schedules 30000 ms retry delay.

impact.operator_impact: Under provider contention, independently-running agents converge retry windows and repeatedly re-enter the provider cooldown interval simultaneously, producing synchronized retry bursts and unstable runtime behavior.
impact.business_consequence: Framework-level retry amplification propagates automatically to downstream agents and workflows, increasing API spend, degrading latency, and creating cascading instability during provider stress or concurrency spikes.

constraints: Retry logic must honor provider Retry-After semantics before scheduling retries.
Framework retry layers must not collapse transient throttling and quota exhaustion into the same retry policy.
Shared-provider runtimes must disperse retry timing rather than synchronize retry windows under contention.
Provider cooldown metadata must survive into retry scheduling decisions.
verification: Setup: Return HTTP 429 with Retry-After: 30 under concurrent multi-agent execution. Action: Trigger framework retry scheduling across multiple agents. Assertion: retries do not synchronize inside the provider cooldown window and no retry occurs before 30000 ms.

Setup: Simulate quota exhaustion and transient throttling responses separately. Action: Execute retry classification path. Assertion: quota exhaustion produces STOP behavior while transient throttling produces WAIT behavior.

Setup: Instrument retry scheduling before and after PR #1283. Action: Compare retry timing distributions under provider contention. Assertion: retry convergence decreases and retry timing disperses according to provider cooldown semantics.
notes: Confirmed via merged PR #1283. The important failure mechanism was not merely Retry-After omission, but synchronized retry amplification emerging at framework scope under shared provider contention. The merged fix introduces Retry-After parsing, retry delay flooring, and validation tests.

constraints: Retry scheduling must honor provider cooldown semantics.
Shared-provider retries must avoid synchronized retry convergence.
Quota exhaustion and transient throttling require distinct retry behavior.
anti_patterns: collapsing quota exhaustion and transient throttling into one retry path
framework-wide synchronized retry scheduling under provider contention
